item.title: Make sure to film the police
item.description: I’m happy that the other cops did the right thing. #police #misconduct #letshopeforaccountability Edit: Thanks to @sambaah05 for sauce. He's a security guard, not a police officer it seems. https://www.nbcchicago.com/news/local/harvey-police-arrest-security-guard-in-video-that-goes-viral/2402429/

item.title: "Oh yeah babe, it's going to take longer than I anticipated though"
item.description: In my defense, I smashed my interview today and after being unemployed for over a month it was a huge boost! Finally feel myself coming out of my depressive episode:)

item.title: Lost my first 20 lbs. Couldn't tell until I took a pic❤️
item.description: 30/5'7/227 lbs first pic 207 lbs second pic goal 160 lbs. Just some good ole calorie counting and walking on the treadmill 1 hour a day 4 days a week?

item.dc:creator: exactchangeman

item.title: Just made my childhood dream come true
item.description: This post will most likely die in the user sub, and this is prob not a big deal to most people. But I always dreamed to score a corner kick since I was little. It took me 1h of practice to get this perfect shot. Edited: Wow, woke up in the morning to find out this made FB, thank everyone for the kind and supportive words. I know it's not that impressive because it's just an empty goal, as some of you pointed out, but for someone who just played soccer for less than a year, this was quite an achievement for me. I'll keep training and score in a match one day. Cheer :)
item.dc:creator: Tientchik

item.title: Cooking the Fried Chicken Recipe
item.description:
This video popped up a few times on Imgur, so I decided to try it out. I've made this four times now and commented asking people if there was interest in seeing the process (https://imgur.com/gallery/AbbtJW8/comment/2215005601). This post has 41 pictures; I'm sorry for the excessive photos. (Create a folder called "Recipes" or "Cooking" to save stuff like this for later) With out further ado, let's get to work. First let's start with what you are going to need:
- *2 bowls for mixing. *not pictured: third bowl for putting flower in. Will be shown later.
- Pot for putting the chicken and brine into. (I used an 8QT/7.6L pot)
- Deep pot for frying.
- One colander to put in the deep frying pot.
- 1/2 teaspoon - 1 teaspoon - 1 Tablespoon - 1 Cup (banana for scale) - A deep fry cooking thermometer - An instant-read digital thermometer (or a meat thermometer) - Tongs - Whisk
- A measuring cup for water
- Chicken Drumsticks
- Sugar - Flour - *Vegetable Oil *Vegetable Oil has a high smoke point of 400° Fahrenheit/204° Celsius, which is needed for this recipe. Also Vegetable Oil helps retain a better flavor; fight me on this.
- 3 eggs
- Paprika - Garlic Powder - Onion Powder - Chinese Five Spice - White Pepper - Dry Mustard Powder - Black Pepper
- Kosher Salt - Bay Leaves - 1 large Orange - Chicken Seasoning - Cloves (not pictured)
MISTAKE #1: I'm used to cooking with garlic clovers. So, when the recipe called for cloves I naturally thought to use garlic. This didn't effect the overall flavor and neither my wife nor I could taste any garlic.
Brine Recipe: - 8 cups of water - 3 tbsp (Tablespoons) kosher salt - 2 tbsp sugar - 3 cloves (not garlic) - 2 bay leaves - *1 orange peel Whisk the ingredients together in the large pot until the kosher salt and sugar are dissolved. *Using a large orange peel (or two orange peels) gives the chicken meat a nice orange aftertaste. I highly recommend going bigger here.
Add the chicken to the pot.
Cover the chicken (tin foil, plastic wrap, or a lid will do).
Refrigerate overnight. (Fast forward 24 hours)
Seasoned Flour Recipe In a mixing bowl add the following: - 2 cups flour - 3 tbsp chicken seasoning - 1/2 tbsp paprika - 1 tbsp garlic powder - 1 tbsp onion powder - 1/2 tbsp black pepper - 1 tsp (teaspoon) Chinese 5 spice - 1 tsp white pepper powder - 1 tsp dry mustard powder - 1-2 pinches kosher salt Mix together.
Batter Mix Recipe In a second mixing bowl, add the following: - 12 fl oz cold water - 3 eggs - 1/2 cup flour Mix together.
Your batter should look similar to this once mixed.
Line up your coating station: Left to right: Seasoned Flour, Batter Mixture, 1-2 cups Flour in a normal bowl. The stove is on the left, so we are going to coat the chicken starting from the right and ending up in the stove on the left.
Place your colander in the large frying pot and add your vegetable oil. Insert your deep frying cooking thermometer.
Heat until the vegetable oil reaches 350° Fahrenheit (176° C).
Wash your hands before handling the chicken. *Remove the chicken from the brine. * I am using one piece to illustrate the process, but it is more efficient to lay out all of the drumsticks together. This helps speed the process up.
Pat chicken dry.
Coat the chicken in flour and shake off the excess flour.
Dip the chicken fully into the batter and shake off the excess batter. Rotate the chicken around if you cannot fully immerse it into the batter.
Coat the chicken in seasoned flour & apply enough pressure to make sure the seasoned flour sticks to the chicken.
Place the chicken into 350° Fahrenheit (176° C) vegetable oil for 16 minutes. *We want the internal temperature of the chicken to reach 165° Fahrenheit (73° C). *For safety reasons, chicken is not cooked until the internal temperature reaches a safe point for human consumption. Do not rely on the look of the chicken; measure the internal temperature with the meat thermometer (don't be a turkey).
The coating process. Music Artist - The Brothers Comatose Song - 120 East https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=teeFu7nZuNw
194° F, well cooked.
How to measure the internal temperature. Turn on your digital thermometer and jab it into the thickest part of the chicken. If the internal temperature is at least 165° F then the chicken is safe to eat.
Did I deep fry the banana? You bet your ass I did (sorry for the lack of a picture). Crispy on the outside, sweet on the inside (2-3 minutes cooked after coating).
Finished Product (honey for dipping)
Excess mix. I'd estimate you could get at least 8-10 drumsticks with the recipe, though the Seasoned Flour might be stretched thin.
No cooking job is complete until the dishes are done and you've cleaned up.
What to do with the excess cooked vegetable oil? It takes at least 1-3 hours to cool down enough to handle. I've read you can refrigerate the used oil for up to a month to use again or freeze it for 3 months. Your mileage may vary. If you aren't interested in saving the oil then I recommend cooking this the day before garbage is collected at your house. You do not want cooking oil to smell up your kitchen or garbage for days, so plan accordingly.
Cat tax (1 of 4) Pod relaxing on his favorite climbing spot. He was born with a half-hind leg that has a pad on it (like on the bottom of a cat's paw). He is still faster than me and climbs the stairs like a cheetah.
Cat tax (2 of 4) Pod admiring his favorite music artist.
Cat tax (3 of 4) Bean resting on the record player, cuddling with her favorite kitty pillow. She has different colored toe beans and one paw with a splash of grey on two of her toes.
Final Cat Tax Pod hamming it up as a kitten. Final notes: 1.) Don't be afraid to make mistakes. Cooking is like making art or practicing science: you learn by your failures. With more practice you should expect to see better results. 2.) Filming with one hand and cooking with the other is difficult; get a mini-tripod (like my cat). 3.) Creating a detailed post can be daunting, and I'm sure I've made a mistake or two. Cest la vie. 4.) I did this post initially on my phone, but had to switch over to my laptop. Lordy the typing involved. 5.) Tagging users is like data entry. Tedious, but appreciated. My wife caught COVID a week ago and I just had my 2nd booster shot yesterday (she is quarantined away from me at our house). This meal is one of her favorites and cheered her up. It was tough concentrating on little things and I almost abandoned filming this, but she insisted I do so as I had made a (semi) promise to the community. I am always happy to contribute. Happy cooking! TL;DR Follow the recipe in the video for delicious deep-fried chicken. @GalleonHeart, @oshea, @Arrow2duhknee, @RickyTickyB, @grandestgrandeur, @Septagon, @Mannus, @whatarewewaitingfor, @m0unstr0, @wpfranner, @Lexiloohoo, @WatDaPho, @thisiskissel, @Starcrested, @DillPixels, @TheWasp, @Deadpo0l, @historymaker121, @Xhristmas, @ImANurse, @TheFifthElementalCase, @FifthCenturion, @L4t3xs, @Nobanob, @Imustupvotealltacos, @Taliwanker, @stereomiljac8888, @beisian, @yifin, @justasleepingcat, @StickyFishDicks, @Xhristmas, @relaxbruv942, @ZealousOtter10, @Rakeinthelake, @KatsukiLaxus, @CHiLL562, @sirweasel, @BloodSweatAndBeers, @CatahoulaBeard, @NationalOrganizationOfMenAgainstAmazonianMasterhood, @DrMoustachio, @Poketummy, @belthasarthegreat, @ijustwanttobuysomeart, @TheWasp, @albinohitchhiker, @TheFirstMemberOfTheDKCrew, @IhavefunonStreetandorinLocalTavern, @NobilisSaeva, @SavgeHnry, @grimCoordinator, @FrauBlucher, @smooalacarte, @TheDungineer, @YavannaBlackwell, @shiviro, @freshage, @Leejama, @LuckyMucky, @tatterBaron, @Mikadomea, @Skyv, @isleepfreely, @StArkyn, @Silentraffic, @Careone, @Vanamun, @BothBothBothBothisgood, @ARoseByAnotherUsernameWouldSmellAsSweet, @zaandari11, @anstasi, @MinimalEfforts, @AMac21net, @Yoshi3, @ItsHedley, @labouka, @Gaelwyn, @redsporky, @oxphmy
